<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Shinshu-kyo]][[ja:神習教]]
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}}
{{afc comment|1=see  for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Shinshu-kyo]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Shinshu-kyo|Shinshu-kyo]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}}

{{AfC submission|t||ts=20230320104955|u=Immanuelle|ns=118|demo=}}

{{notability|date=March 2023}}

{{nihongo|{{ill|Shinshu-kyo|ja|神習教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshu-kyo|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|神習教}} is a [[Sect Shinto]] organization and one of the thirteen shinto sects.<ref>{{Cite book |last=井上 |first=順孝 |title= |date=April 1991 |publisher=弘文堂 |isbn=978-4335160219 |page=11 |script-title=ja:教派神道の形成 |author-link=井上順孝}}</ref><ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Kyōha Shintō {{!}} Japanese religion |url=https://www.britannica.com/topic/Kyoha-Shinto |access-date=2023-03-06 |website=Encyclopedia Britannica |language=en}}</ref>

It was founded by Masaki Yoshimura<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |last=小項目事典 |first=朝日日本歴史人物事典,デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 |title=芳村正秉(よしむら・まさもち)とは？ 意味や使い方 |website=コトバンク |language=ja |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%8A%B3%E6%9D%91%E6%AD%A3%E7%A7%89-146230 |access-date=2023-03-06}}</ref> who lived from 1839-1915<ref>{{Cite web |title=歴史 |url=http://shinshukyo.jp/%e6%ad%b4%e5%8f%b2/ |access-date=2023-03-09 |website=神道神習教公式ページ |language=ja}}</ref> He was a survivor of the [[:en:Ansei Purge|Ansei Purge]].{{sfn|井上順孝|1991|p=277-278}} and worked at [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Jingu]] and later was head of [[Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Shrine]], but due to laws restricting teaching he entrusted his children to [[Itō Hirobumi]] (before he became Prime Minister) and established a new branch based on his {{ill|family knowledge|ja|家学|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/family knowledge|qq=}}{{sfn|井上順孝|1991|p=282}}

It is considered a "purification sect" alongside [[Misogikyo]].<ref name=":0" />

== References ==
{{reflist}}

== Bibliography ==
* {{Cite book |author=井上順孝 |title=教派神道の形成 |date=1991 |publisher=[[:ja:弘文堂|弘文堂]] |authorlink=:ja:井上順孝}}

{{Sect Shinto}}

{{Drafts moved from mainspace|date=March 2023}}

}}

{{nihongo|{{ill|Shinshu-kyo|ja|神習教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshu-kyo|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|神習教}} is a [[Sect Shinto]] organization and one of the thirteen shinto sects.<ref>{{Cite book |last=井上 |first=順孝 |title= |date=April 1991 |publisher=弘文堂 |isbn=978-4335160219 |page=11 |script-title=ja:教派神道の形成 |author-link=井上順孝}}</ref><ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Kyōha Shintō {{!}} Japanese religion |url=https://www.britannica.com/topic/Kyoha-Shinto |access-date=2023-03-06 |website=Encyclopedia Britannica |language=en}}</ref>

It was founded by Masaki Yoshimura<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |last=小項目事典 |first=朝日日本歴史人物事典,デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 |title=芳村正秉(よしむら・まさもち)とは？ 意味や使い方 |website=コトバンク |language=ja |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%8A%B3%E6%9D%91%E6%AD%A3%E7%A7%89-146230 |access-date=2023-03-06}}</ref> who lived from 1839-1915<ref>{{Cite web |title=歴史 |url=http://shinshukyo.jp/%e6%ad%b4%e5%8f%b2/ |access-date=2023-03-09 |website=神道神習教公式ページ |language=ja}}</ref> He was a survivor of the [[:en:Ansei Purge|Ansei Purge]].{{sfn|井上順孝|1991|p=277-278}} and worked at [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Jingu]] and later was head of [[Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Shrine]], but due to laws restricting teaching he entrusted his children to [[Itō Hirobumi]] (before he became Prime Minister) and established a new branch based on his {{ill|family knowledge|ja|家学|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/family knowledge|qq=}}{{sfn|井上順孝|1991|p=282}}

It is considered a "purification sect" alongside [[Misogikyo]].<ref name=":0" />

{{nihongo|{{ill|Shinshu-kyo|ja|神習教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshu-kyo|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|神習教}} is a [[Sect Shinto]] organization and one of the thirteen shinto sects.<ref>{{Cite book |last=井上 |first=順孝 |title= |date=April 1991 |publisher=弘文堂 |isbn=978-4335160219 |page=11 |script-title=ja:教派神道の形成 |author-link=井上順孝}}</ref><ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Kyōha Shintō {{!}} Japanese religion |url=https://www.britannica.com/topic/Kyoha-Shinto |access-date=2023-03-06 |website=Encyclopedia Britannica |language=en}}</ref>

It was founded by Masaki Yoshimura<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |last=小項目事典 |first=朝日日本歴史人物事典,デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 |title=芳村正秉(よしむら・まさもち)とは？ 意味や使い方 |website=コトバンク |language=ja |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%8A%B3%E6%9D%91%E6%AD%A3%E7%A7%89-146230 |access-date=2023-03-06}}</ref> who lived from 1839-1915<ref>{{Cite web |title=歴史 |url=http://shinshukyo.jp/%e6%ad%b4%e5%8f%b2/ |access-date=2023-03-09 |website=神道神習教公式ページ |language=ja}}</ref> He was a survivor of the [[:en:Ansei Purge|Ansei Purge]].{{sfn|井上順孝|1991|p=277-278}} and worked at [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Jingu]] and later was head of [[Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Shrine]], but due to laws restricting teaching he entrusted his children to [[Itō Hirobumi]] (before he became Prime Minister) and established a new branch based on his {{ill|family knowledge|ja|家学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/family knowledge|qq=}}{{sfn|井上順孝|1991|p=282}}

It is considered a "purification sect" alongside [[Misogikyo]].<ref name=":0" />

{{notability|date=March 2023}}

{{nihongo|{{ill|Shinshu-kyo|ja|神習教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshu-kyo|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|神習教}} is a [[Sect Shinto]] organization and one of the thirteen shinto sects.<ref>{{Cite book |last=井上 |first=順孝 |title= |date=April 1991 |publisher=弘文堂 |isbn=978-4335160219 |page=11 |script-title=ja:教派神道の形成 |author-link=井上順孝}}</ref><ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Kyōha Shintō {{!}} Japanese religion |url=https://www.britannica.com/topic/Kyoha-Shinto |access-date=2023-03-06 |website=Encyclopedia Britannica |language=en}}</ref>

It was founded by Masaki Yoshimura<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |last=小項目事典 |first=朝日日本歴史人物事典,デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 |title=芳村正秉(よしむら・まさもち)とは？ 意味や使い方 |website=コトバンク |language=ja |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%8A%B3%E6%9D%91%E6%AD%A3%E7%A7%89-146230 |access-date=2023-03-06}}</ref> who lived from 1839-1915<ref>{{Cite web |title=歴史 |url=http://shinshukyo.jp/%e6%ad%b4%e5%8f%b2/ |access-date=2023-03-09 |website=神道神習教公式ページ |language=ja}}</ref> He was a survivor of the [[:en:Ansei Purge|Ansei Purge]].{{sfn|井上順孝|1991|p=277-278}} and worked at [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Jingu]] and later was head of [[Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Shrine]], but due to laws restricting teaching he entrusted his children to [[Itō Hirobumi]] (before he became Prime Minister) and established a new branch based on his {{ill|family knowledge|ja|家学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/family knowledge|qq=}}{{sfn|井上順孝|1991|p=282}}

It is considered a "purification sect" alongside [[Misogikyo]].<ref name=":0" />

{{AfC submission|t||ts=20230320104955|u=Immanuelle|ns=118|demo=}}

{{notability|date=March 2023}}

{{nihongo|{{ill|Shinshu-kyo|ja|神習教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshu-kyo|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|神習教}} is a [[Sect Shinto]] organization and one of the thirteen shinto sects.<ref>{{Cite book |last=井上 |first=順孝 |title= |date=April 1991 |publisher=弘文堂 |isbn=978-4335160219 |page=11 |script-title=ja:教派神道の形成 |author-link=井上順孝}}</ref><ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Kyōha Shintō {{!}} Japanese religion |url=https://www.britannica.com/topic/Kyoha-Shinto |access-date=2023-03-06 |website=Encyclopedia Britannica |language=en}}</ref>

It was founded by Masaki Yoshimura<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |last=小項目事典 |first=朝日日本歴史人物事典,デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 |title=芳村正秉(よしむら・まさもち)とは？ 意味や使い方 |website=コトバンク |language=ja |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%8A%B3%E6%9D%91%E6%AD%A3%E7%A7%89-146230 |access-date=2023-03-06}}</ref> who lived from 1839-1915<ref>{{Cite web |title=歴史 |url=http://shinshukyo.jp/%e6%ad%b4%e5%8f%b2/ |access-date=2023-03-09 |website=神道神習教公式ページ |language=ja}}</ref> He was a survivor of the [[:en:Ansei Purge|Ansei Purge]].{{sfn|井上順孝|1991|p=277-278}} and worked at [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Jingu]] and later was head of [[Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Shrine]], but due to laws restricting teaching he entrusted his children to [[Itō Hirobumi]] (before he became Prime Minister) and established a new branch based on his {{ill|family knowledge|ja|家学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/family knowledge|qq=}}{{sfn|井上順孝|1991|p=282}}

It is considered a "purification sect" alongside [[Misogikyo]].<ref name=":0" />

It was founded by Masaki Yoshimura<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |last=小項目事典 |first=朝日日本歴史人物事典,デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 |title=芳村正秉(よしむら・まさもち)とは？ 意味や使い方 |website=コトバンク |language=ja |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%8A%B3%E6%9D%91%E6%AD%A3%E7%A7%89-146230 |access-date=2023-03-06}}</ref> who lived from 1839-1915<ref>{{Cite web |title=歴史 |url=http://shinshukyo.jp/%e6%ad%b4%e5%8f%b2/ |access-date=2023-03-09 |website=神道神習教公式ページ |language=ja}}</ref> He was a survivor of the [[:en:Ansei Purge|Ansei Purge]].{{sfn|井上順孝|1991|p=277-278}} and worked at [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Jingu]] and later was head of [[Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Shrine]], but due to laws restricting teaching he entrusted his children to [[Itō Hirobumi]] (before he became Prime Minister) and established a new branch based on his {{ill|family knowledge|ja|家学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/family knowledge|qq=}}{{sfn|井上順孝|1991|p=282}}

It is considered a "purification sect" alongside [[Misogikyo]].<ref name=":0" />

{{notability|date=March 2023}}

{{nihongo|{{ill|Shinshu-kyo|ja|神習教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshu-kyo|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|神習教}} is a [[Sect Shinto]] organization and one of the thirteen shinto sects.<ref>{{Cite book |last=井上 |first=順孝 |title= |date=April 1991 |publisher=弘文堂 |isbn=978-4335160219 |page=11 |script-title=ja:教派神道の形成 |author-link=井上順孝}}</ref><ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Kyōha Shintō {{!}} Japanese religion |url=https://www.britannica.com/topic/Kyoha-Shinto |access-date=2023-03-06 |website=Encyclopedia Britannica |language=en}}</ref>

It was founded by Masaki Yoshimura<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |last=小項目事典 |first=朝日日本歴史人物事典,デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 |title=芳村正秉(よしむら・まさもち)とは？ 意味や使い方 |website=コトバンク |language=ja |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%8A%B3%E6%9D%91%E6%AD%A3%E7%A7%89-146230 |access-date=2023-03-06}}</ref> who lived from 1839-1915<ref>{{Cite web |title=歴史 |url=http://shinshukyo.jp/%e6%ad%b4%e5%8f%b2/ |access-date=2023-03-09 |website=神道神習教公式ページ |language=ja}}</ref> He was a survivor of the [[:en:Ansei Purge|Ansei Purge]].{{sfn|井上順孝|1991|p=277-278}} and worked at [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Jingu]] and later was head of [[Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Shrine]], but due to laws restricting teaching he entrusted his children to [[Itō Hirobumi]] (before he became Prime Minister) and established a new branch based on his {{ill|family knowledge|ja|家学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/family knowledge|qq=}}{{sfn|井上順孝|1991|p=282}}

It is considered a "purification sect" alongside [[Misogikyo]].<ref name=":0" />

{{nihongo|{{ill|Shinshu-kyo|ja|神習教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshu-kyo|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|神習教}} is a [[Sect Shinto]] organization and one of the thirteen shinto sects.<ref>{{Cite book |last=井上 |first=順孝 |title= |date=April 1991 |publisher=弘文堂 |isbn=978-4335160219 |page=11 |script-title=ja:教派神道の形成 |author-link=井上順孝}}</ref><ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Kyōha Shintō {{!}} Japanese religion |url=https://www.britannica.com/topic/Kyoha-Shinto |access-date=2023-03-06 |website=Encyclopedia Britannica |language=en}}</ref>

It was founded by Masaki Yoshimura<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |last=小項目事典 |first=朝日日本歴史人物事典,デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 |title=芳村正秉(よしむら・まさもち)とは？ 意味や使い方 |website=コトバンク |language=ja |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%8A%B3%E6%9D%91%E6%AD%A3%E7%A7%89-146230 |access-date=2023-03-06}}</ref> who lived from 1839-1915<ref>{{Cite web |title=歴史 |url=http://shinshukyo.jp/%e6%ad%b4%e5%8f%b2/ |access-date=2023-03-09 |website=神道神習教公式ページ |language=ja}}</ref> He was a survivor of the {{ill|Ansei Purge|en|Ansei Purge|WD=Q569487|lt=Ansei Purge}}.{{sfn|井上順孝|1991|p=277-278}} and worked at [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Jingu]] and later was head of [[Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Shrine]], but due to laws restricting teaching he entrusted his children to [[Itō Hirobumi]] (before he became Prime Minister) and established a new branch based on his {{ill|family knowledge|ja|家学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/family knowledge|qq=}}{{sfn|井上順孝|1991|p=282}}

It is considered a "purification sect" alongside [[Misogikyo]].<ref name=":0" />

== References ==
{{reflist}}

== Bibliography ==
* {{Cite book |author=井上順孝 |title=教派神道の形成 |date=1991 |publisher=[[:ja:弘文堂|弘文堂]] |authorlink=:ja:井上順孝}}

It was founded by Masaki Yoshimura<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |last=小項目事典 |first=朝日日本歴史人物事典,デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 |title=芳村正秉(よしむら・まさもち)とは？ 意味や使い方 |website=コトバンク |language=ja |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%8A%B3%E6%9D%91%E6%AD%A3%E7%A7%89-146230 |access-date=2023-03-06}}</ref> who lived from 1839-1915<ref>{{Cite web |title=歴史 |url=http://shinshukyo.jp/%e6%ad%b4%e5%8f%b2/ |access-date=2023-03-09 |website=神道神習教公式ページ |language=ja}}</ref> He was a survivor of the {{ill|Ansei Purge|en|Ansei Purge|WD=Q569487|lt=Ansei Purge}}.{{sfn|井上順孝|1991|p=277-278}} and worked at [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Jingu]] and later was head of [[Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Shrine]], but due to laws restricting teaching he entrusted his children to [[Itō Hirobumi]] (before he became Prime Minister) and established a new branch based on his {{ill|family knowledge|ja|家学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/family knowledge|qq=}}{{sfn|井上順孝|1991|p=282}}

It is considered a "purification sect" alongside [[Misogikyo]].<ref name=":0" />

{{nihongo|{{ill|Shinshu-kyo|ja|神習教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshu-kyo|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|神習教}} is a [[Sect Shinto]] organization and one of the thirteen shinto sects.<ref>{{Cite book |last=井上 |first=順孝 |title= |date=April 1991 |publisher=弘文堂 |isbn=978-4335160219 |page=11 |script-title=ja:教派神道の形成 |author-link=井上順孝}}</ref><ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Kyōha Shintō {{!}} Japanese religion |url=https://www.britannica.com/topic/Kyoha-Shinto |access-date=2023-03-06 |website=Encyclopedia Britannica |language=en}}</ref>

It was founded by Masaki Yoshimura<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |last=小項目事典 |first=朝日日本歴史人物事典,デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 |title=芳村正秉(よしむら・まさもち)とは？ 意味や使い方 |website=コトバンク |language=ja |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%8A%B3%E6%9D%91%E6%AD%A3%E7%A7%89-146230 |access-date=2023-03-06}}</ref> who lived from 1839-1915<ref>{{Cite web |title=歴史 |url=http://shinshukyo.jp/%e6%ad%b4%e5%8f%b2/ |access-date=2023-03-09 |website=神道神習教公式ページ |language=ja}}</ref> He was a survivor of the {{ill|Ansei Purge|en|Ansei Purge|WD=Q569487|lt=Ansei Purge|1=Ansei Purge}}.{{sfn|井上順孝|1991|p=277-278}} and worked at [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Jingu]] and later was head of [[Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Shrine]], but due to laws restricting teaching he entrusted his children to [[Itō Hirobumi]] (before he became Prime Minister) and established a new branch based on his {{ill|family knowledge|ja|家学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/family knowledge|qq=}}{{sfn|井上順孝|1991|p=282}}

It is considered a "purification sect" alongside [[Misogikyo]].<ref name=":0" />
